---
title: "Assignment 01: Brush up R and Quarto"
format: html
---

## EPPS 6323 – Knowledge Mining  
**Assignment 01**

This assignment reviews basic R programming and performs exploratory data analysis (EDA) using the **TEDS2016 dataset**.

---

## 1. Review of R Programming

To refresh R programming skills, the following resources were reviewed:

- Introduction to R (University of Southern California)
- EPPS Math and Coding Camp materials
- Chapters 3–7 of *R for Data Science (R4DS)*

---

## 2. Lab Exercises

The following lab exercises were completed and published on this website.

- [Lab 01: R Programming Basics I](Lab01.html)
- [Lab 02: R Programming Basics II](Lab02.html)

---

## 3. Load the TEDS2016 Dataset

The dataset was loaded directly from GitHub using the **haven** package.

```{r}
library(haven)

TEDS_2016 <- haven::read_dta(
"https://github.com/datageneration/home/blob/master/DataProgramming/data/TEDS_2016.dta?raw=true"
)

head(TEDS_2016)

Problems encountered when working with the dataset

Several issues were encountered:

Missing values in multiple variables

Some variables are stored as labelled values

Need to convert variables to numeric or factor types before analysis

Understanding variable meanings required consulting documentation

These issues required cleaning and transforming the dataset before analysis.

---
  
## 3. Handling Missing Values

TEDS_clean <- na.omit(TEDS_2016)

TEDS_2016$Tondu <- as.numeric(TEDS_2016$Tondu)

table(TEDS_2016$Tondu)

table(TEDS_2016$Tondu)

barplot(
table(TEDS_2016$Tondu),
main = "Distribution of Tondu",
xlab = "Tondu Categories",
ylab = "Frequency"
)
